info.name: Module 3 — Web Server + PKCE (Salesforce + Dropbox)
info.description:
Two folders:

1. 'Inbound — Salesforce (as yourself)': first get a token via Postman's OAuth helper (NOT a request — see below), then run the requests.
2. 'Project — Dropbox': Session A of the Document Filer project — consent, list, upload, share.

HOW TO GET THE SALESFORCE TOKEN (Lab 1):
Collection > Authorization tab > OAuth 2.0 > Configure New Token:
- Grant type: Authorization Code (With PKCE)
- Callback URL: https://oauth.pstmn.io/v1/callback  (must be on the ECA's Callback URL list)
- Auth URL: {{my_domain}}/services/oauth2/authorize
- Access Token URL: {{my_domain}}/services/oauth2/token
- Client ID / Secret: ECA consumer key + secret
- Code Challenge Method: SHA-256
- Scope: api refresh_token
Get New Access Token > log in > Allow > copy tokens into the variables access_token / refresh_token.

HOW TO GET THE DROPBOX TOKEN (Project Session A):
Same helper on the 'Project — Dropbox' folder:
- Auth URL: https://www.dropbox.com/oauth2/authorize?token_access_type=offline   ← offline = refresh token!
- Access Token URL: https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token
- Client ID / Secret: Dropbox App key / App secret
- Code Challenge Method: SHA-256 · Scope: leave empty (Dropbox uses the app's Permissions tab)
Save the token into dropbox_access_token.

Variables to set: my_domain, consumer_key, consumer_secret (Salesforce); the Dropbox tokens come from the helper.

item.name: Inbound — Salesforce (as yourself)
item.description: Run after completing the OAuth-helper login described in the collection description. These calls act as YOU — compare with Module 2's collection where the same endpoints act as the integration user.

request.description: Returns YOUR name/email/user id. Same endpoint with a Module 2 client-credentials token returns the integration user — same org, different flow, different identity.

query.value: SELECT Id, Name FROM Account LIMIT 5

request.description: Runs with YOUR permissions and sharing — an admin sees more than Module 2's least-privilege integration user did.

exec: console.log('Rotation is ON — new refresh token saved.');
console.log('Fresh access token saved (rotation off — keep the old refresh token).');

request.description: The refresh token flow by hand, so it's never magic: fresh access token, no browser, no login. This is how mobile apps stay signed in for months.

item.name: Project — Dropbox
item.description: Document Filer, Session A. Get the token via the OAuth helper first (see collection description — remember token_access_type=offline on the Auth URL, and leave Scope empty).

request.description: Dropbox RPC endpoints are POST-based. Returns the connected account — proof the consent worked.
Empty path "" = the app folder's root (App-folder access type keeps the integration in its own sandbox folder).

item.name: 3. Upload a file (NOTE: content host + header args)

body.raw: Hello Dropbox — this body is the raw file content.

request.description: Two things to notice: (1) the host is content.dropboxapi.com — NOT api. — because content endpoints live on their own host; (2) the JSON parameters ride in the Dropbox-API-Arg HEADER because the body is busy carrying the file bytes. Header must be ASCII-safe.
The response's 'url' opens the file in any browser. Run it twice to meet 409 shared_link_already_exists — links are create-once (the Apex service handles this by fetching the existing link).
